name: Pai Honolulu
tagline: Hidden courtyard fine dining near Aloha Tower — chef-owned, locally sourced, and genuinely hard to find unless someone tells you.
mustOrder: seasonal tasting menu — changes regularly based on local sourcing
a la carte dishes — everything is made in-house
ask chef Kevin Lee what's best that night
parking: Harbor Court parking; street parking on Merchant St
talkStory: Chef Kevin Lee opened Pai in 2017, tucked in Harbor Court near Aloha Tower — the hidden courtyard entrance is intentionally hard to find. Lee, who trained under Mavro and other top Honolulu chefs, changes the tasting menu weekly based on what small Hawaiian farms deliver. The name 'Pai' references the Hawaiian concept of eating communally. Everything from pasta to bread to charcuterie is made in-house. One of Honolulu's most under-the-radar fine dining spots — no Instagram hype, just craft.
